title: Grafana Alerting Notification Channel Delivery Failures (Unified Alerting contact points Slack Teams PagerDuty Opsgenie webhook SMTP Discord MSGraph email-list OSS Enterprise Cloud legacy channels correlation)
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.6.2 data source query error analytics, UC-13.3.5 alert storm deduplication economics, per-panel SLO burn calculators in isolation, or secure software supply chain attestations unrelated to notifier delivery. Does not auto-mute Grafana alert rules; it produces evidence for governed remediation of contact points and secrets.
evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_6_13_grafana_contact_point_delivery_failures on a five-minute schedule; observability dashboard drilldowns across Grafana server, audit, metrics, and provider sourcetypes; alert routing to platform observability on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab Grafana stack, configure a contact point with an invalid webhook URL or revoked token, run Send test notification, and confirm observability_uc_13_6_13_grafana_contact_point_delivery_failures returns critical or high within two five-minute windows with non-null recommended_action and populated contact_point_uid.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After restoring valid credentials, successful test notifications, quiet provider mirrors, and balanced Grafana alerting metrics, confirm severities remain low for steady state and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only for thirty consecutive runs.

dataSources: `index=grafana` or `index=observability` or `index=platform` with `sourcetype=grafana:server` for `lvl=eror` lines and `logger=ngalert` notifier failures; `sourcetype=grafana:audit` or `sourcetype=grafana:json` for `notification_send` and contact point lifecycle actions; `index=metrics` or `observability_metrics` or `prometheus_metrics` with `sourcetype=prometheus:metrics` for `grafana_alerting_notifications_total`, `grafana_alerting_notifications_failed_total`, and `grafana_alerting_state_history_dropped_total`; `index=observability` or `index=alerts_ingest` with `sourcetype=pagerduty:webhook`, `sourcetype=slack:audit`, or `sourcetype=opsgenie:audit` for downstream acknowledgement mirrors; lookups `grafana_contact_point_inventory.csv` and `index=catalog` `sourcetype=integration_secret_inventory`.
app: Grafana OSS Enterprise Cloud plus Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector or Universal Forwarder log pipelines and Prometheus metrics scrape into Splunk
spl:
`comment("UC-13.6.13 Grafana Unified Alerting contact point delivery failures correlation ngalert notifier logs audit notification_send metrics provider arms earliest=-4h@h bucket=5m streamstats_window=2_contact_point")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=main) sourcetype=grafana:server earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where (like(lr,"%lvl=eror%") OR like(lr,"%level=error%") OR like(lr,"%lvl=error%")) AND (like(lr,"%ngalert%") OR like(lr,"%notifier%") OR match(lr,"notifier failed|failed to send notification|retrying notification|contact point|receiver"))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval contact_point_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_uid,contactPointUid,receiver_uid,receiverUID,uid,"cp_unknown")))
      | eval contact_point_name=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_name,receiver_name,name,"unknown_cp")))
      | eval contact_point_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_type,receiver_type,integration_type,type,"webhook"))))
      | eval alert_rule_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_uid,alertRuleUID,alert_rule_uid_grafana,ruleUID,"rule_unknown")))
      | eval notifications_total=1
      | eval notifications_failed=if(match(lr,"failed|error sending|could not send|invalid|unauthor|forbidden|expired|revoked|rate limit|too many requests|timeout|deadline exceeded|4[0-9]{2}|5[0-9]{2}"),1,0)
      | rex field=lr max_match=1 "(?i)\b(?<rex_http>401|403|404|410|413|422|429|500|502|503)\b"
      | eval top_status_code=coalesce(trim(toString(status)),rex_http,"log_err")
      | eval avg_latency_ms=if(match(lr,"duration|latency|took|ms"),200.0,95.0)
      | eval integration_token_age_days=null()
      | eval lane="grafana_ngalert_log"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform) (sourcetype=grafana:audit OR sourcetype=grafana:json) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval act=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(action,verb,operation,""))))
      | where match(act,"notification|contact.point|alerting|receiver|notifier") OR match(lr,"notification_send|contact.point|receiver")
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval contact_point_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_uid,resource_uid,uid,resourceId,"audit_cp")))
      | eval contact_point_name=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_name,name,"audit_cp_name")))
      | eval contact_point_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_type,kind,channel_type,"audit_type"))))
      | eval alert_rule_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(alert_rule_uid,rule_uid,ruleUID,"audit_rule")))
      | eval notifications_total=1
      | eval notifications_failed=if(match(lr,"fail|error|denied|unauthor|forbidden|invalid|expired"),1,0)
      | eval top_status_code=trim(toString(coalesce(status,statusCode,http_status,result_code,"audit")))
      | eval avg_latency_ms=tonumber(trim(tostring(coalesce(duration_ms,elapsed_ms,latency_ms,"88"))),10)
      | eval integration_token_age_days=null()
      | eval lane="grafana_audit_notification"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=prometheus_metrics OR index=otel) (sourcetype=prometheus:metrics OR sourcetype=statsd) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"grafana_alerting_notifications_total|grafana_alerting_notifications_failed_total|grafana_alerting_state_history_dropped_total")
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,orgId,org,"0")))
      | eval contact_point_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_uid,receiver,uid,receiver_uid,"metric_cp")))
      | eval contact_point_name="metric_contact_point"
      | eval contact_point_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(receiver_type,type,integration,backend,"metrics_lane"))))
      | eval alert_rule_uid="metric_rule_bucket"
      | eval val=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(Value,value,metric_value,"0")),10)
      | eval notifications_failed=if(match(mn,"failed_total|dropped_total"),val,0)
      | eval notifications_total=if(mn=="grafana_alerting_notifications_total",val,if(match(mn,"failed_total|dropped_total"),val,0))
      | eval notifications_total=if(notifications_total<1 AND notifications_failed>0,notifications_failed,notifications_total)
      | eval notifications_total=if(notifications_total<1,1,notifications_total)
      | eval top_status_code="metric_series"
      | eval avg_latency_ms=130.0
      | eval integration_token_age_days=null()
      | eval lane="grafana_alert_metrics"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats sum(notifications_total) AS notifications_total sum(notifications_failed) AS notifications_failed max(avg_latency_ms) AS avg_latency_ms BY _time org_id contact_point_uid contact_point_name contact_point_type alert_rule_uid top_status_code lane ]
    [ search (index=observability OR index=alerts_ingest OR index=web OR index=platform) (sourcetype=pagerduty:webhook OR sourcetype=slack:audit OR sourcetype=opsgenie:audit) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,orgId,org,"0")))
      | eval contact_point_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_uid,integration_id,channel_id,webhook_id,"ext_cp")))
      | eval contact_point_name=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_name,summary,title,alias,"ext_name")))
      | eval contact_point_type=case(sourcetype=="pagerduty:webhook","pagerduty", sourcetype=="slack:audit","slack", true(),"opsgenie")
      | eval alert_rule_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(alert_rule_uid,dedup_key,incident_key,messageId,"ext_rule")))
      | eval notifications_total=1
      | eval notifications_failed=if(match(lr,"401|403|404|410|429|500|502|503|invalid|expired|rate|denied|unauthor|forbidden|retry_after"),1,0)
      | rex field=lr max_match=1 "(?i)\b(?<rex_http2>401|403|404|429|500|502|503)\b"
      | eval top_status_code=coalesce(trim(toString(coalesce(http_status,status,response_code))),rex_http2,"provider_ok")
      | eval avg_latency_ms=tonumber(trim(tostring(coalesce(response_time_ms,elapsed_ms,request_time_ms,"105"))),10)
      | eval integration_token_age_days=null()
      | eval lane="downstream_provider_mirror"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
| eval org_id=coalesce(org_id,"0")
| eval contact_point_uid=coalesce(contact_point_uid,"unknown_cp")
| eval contact_point_name=coalesce(contact_point_name,"unknown_cp_name")
| eval contact_point_type=lower(coalesce(contact_point_type,"unknown_type"))
| eval alert_rule_uid=coalesce(alert_rule_uid,"unknown_rule")
| eval top_status_code=coalesce(top_status_code,"none")
| stats sum(notifications_total) AS notifications_total sum(notifications_failed) AS notifications_failed max(avg_latency_ms) AS avg_latency_ms values(top_status_code) AS status_vals BY _time org_id contact_point_uid contact_point_name contact_point_type alert_rule_uid lane
| eval top_status_code=mvindex(mvdedup(status_vals),0)
| eval failure_rate=round(notifications_failed/nullif(notifications_total,0),4)
| sort 0 + org_id + contact_point_uid + alert_rule_uid + _time
| streamstats window=2 current=t global=f sum(notifications_failed) AS win_fail sum(notifications_total) AS win_tot BY contact_point_uid org_id
| eval roll_fail_rate=round(win_fail/nullif(win_tot,0),4)
| eventstats avg(notifications_total) AS org_notif_baseline stdev(notifications_total) AS org_notif_std BY org_id
| eval integration_token_age_days=coalesce(integration_token_age_days,0)
| eval severity=case(
    (roll_fail_rate>0.3 AND win_tot>=4) OR match(top_status_code,"401|403") OR (integration_token_age_days>90 AND notifications_failed>0), "critical",
    roll_fail_rate>0.15 OR notifications_failed>=3 OR match(top_status_code,"429|500|502|503"), "high",
    roll_fail_rate>0.05 OR notifications_failed>=1, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    match(top_status_code,"401|403"), "rotate_oauth_and_webhook_secrets_reauthorize_msgraph_teams_workflows_validate_scopes",
    match(top_status_code,"429"), "backoff_notifier_retry_tune_rate_limits_pagerduty_events_api_v3_discord_webhooks",
    match(top_status_code,"500|502|503"), "validate_provider_outage_tls_interception_smtp_relay_dkim_dmarc_alignment",
    match(contact_point_type,"smtp|email"), "inspect_smtp_relay_cert_chain_rfc6125_hostname_verify_and_dmarc_reports",
    roll_fail_rate>0.3, "page_platform_silence_risk_contact_point_broken_rule_may_fire_without_delivery",
    true(), "open_grafana_contact_point_test_notification_replay_audit_notification_send")
| join type=left max=0 contact_point_uid
    [| inputlookup grafana_contact_point_inventory.csv
     | eval contact_point_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_uid,uid,"")))
     | fields contact_point_uid org owner_team on_call_role sla_minutes]
| join type=left max=0 contact_point_type
    [| search index=catalog sourcetype=integration_secret_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now
     | dedup contact_point_type
     | eval contact_point_type=lower(trim(toString(contact_point_type)))
     | fields contact_point_type secret_age_days oauth_scope_drift_flag]
| eval integration_token_age_days=if(isnotnull(secret_age_days) AND secret_age_days>0,secret_age_days,integration_token_age_days)
| eval recommended_action=case(
    match(tostring(oauth_scope_drift_flag),"1"), "remediate_msgraph_teams_permission_scope_drift_reconsent_integration",
    true(), recommended_action)
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned_route")
| table _time, org_id, contact_point_uid, contact_point_name, contact_point_type, alert_rule_uid, notifications_total, notifications_failed, failure_rate, top_status_code, avg_latency_ms, integration_token_age_days, severity, recommended_action, owner_team
description: Detects Grafana Unified Alerting and legacy-channel notification delivery failures across Slack, Teams, PagerDuty, Opsgenie, generic webhooks, SMTP and email-list receivers, Discord, and Microsoft Graph oriented contact points by correlating ngalert notifier errors, audit notification_send outcomes, native Grafana alerting metrics, and downstream provider HTTP evidence so alert rules that fire still reach on-call paths.
value: Reliability leaders catch silent paging breakage before incidents go unheard: they see which contact point UIDs fail, which HTTP status classes repeat, whether OAuth or webhook secrets went stale, and which owner team must rotate credentials or replace deprecated Teams connectors, while audit-ready exports prove the control operated during governance reviews.
implementation: Land `grafana:server`, `grafana:audit` or `grafana:json`, Prometheus-style Grafana alerting metrics, and provider mirror sourcetypes into observability indexes; publish `grafana_contact_point_inventory.csv` and `integration_secret_inventory` catalog feeds; save `observability_uc_13_6_13_grafana_contact_point_delivery_failures` on a five-minute cadence with earliest four hours; route critical and high severities to platform observability on-call; archive weekly evidence CSV snapshots to a restricted index.
visualization: Heatmap of `failure_rate` by `contact_point_type` and `org_id`, severity-colored table with drilldowns to raw server, audit, metrics, and provider rows, single-value tiles for rolling `roll_fail_rate`, timeline of `avg_latency_ms` when present.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Alerts.signature) AS sig latest(Alerts.severity) AS sev count FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.dest
| rename Alerts.dest AS alert_dest
| join type=left max=0 alert_dest [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Web.status) AS web_status latest(Web.url) AS web_url FROM datamodel=Web WHERE nodename=Web earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Web.src
| rename Web.src AS alert_dest]
| join type=left max=0 alert_dest [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Authentication.user) AS auth_user FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE nodename=Authentication earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Authentication.src
| rename Authentication.src AS alert_dest]
| table alert_dest sig sev web_status web_url auth_user
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Alerts summaries for notifier-adjacent alert objects when mapped to CIM Alerts; accelerate Web summaries for outbound webhook HTTP status context; accelerate Authentication summaries for OAuth session and grant events correlated to Grafana identities; retain thirty days for quarterly observability attestations.

references.title: Microsoft Learn — Microsoft Graph REST API v1.0 reference
RFC 6125 — Representation and Verification of Domain-Based Application Service Identity

knownFalsePositives: Planned integration tests that intentionally point contact points at broken lab endpoints will raise failure_rate without customer impact; tag lab org identifiers and suppress production pages. Contact point migrations from legacy notification channels to Unified Alerting receivers produce short bursts of notifier errors during cutover; correlate with change tickets before escalating. Scheduled secret rotation windows can spike HTTP 401 responses for minutes until new credentials propagate; use integration_secret_inventory rotation metadata to downshift severity during approved windows. Sandbox webhooks reused from developer laptops may generate noisy HTTP 400 classes unrelated to production routes; segregate indexes or sourcetypes for non-production. Webhook receiver services under scheduled maintenance may return HTTP 500 series to Grafana while applications remain healthy; overlay service maintenance calendars. Discord and Slack global incidents can elevate HTTP 429 rows across many tenants simultaneously; compare vendor status before internal blame. Email-list receivers may succeed at SMTP handoff yet fail at recipient gateways; pair this control with mail delivery analytics to avoid false confidence. Grafana Cloud capacity events can raise metric-lane noise without misconfiguration; validate with vendor health. Penetration testing tools that probe webhook URLs can create artificial failures; maintain test IP allowlists where possible. Duplicate scrapers of Grafana metrics can inflate notifications_total denominators; dedupe Prometheus targets architecturally.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — group episodes by org and contact_point_uid entities when alert episodes consume Grafana-backed notifier evidence

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability engineers who operate Grafana fleets, the identity teams that mint OAuth clients for Microsoft Graph and Slack, the collaboration administrators who govern Teams workflows, and the incident tooling owners who curate PagerDuty and Opsgenie integrations. This use case is deliberately not UC-13.6.2, which chases data source query failures and blank panels; here the panels can be healthy while on-call silence is lethal because Unified Alerting attempted delivery and the contact point path broke. It is also not UC-13.3.5, which measures alert storms and deduplication economics across many firing signals; a single quiet routing failure can hide under calm global alert volume while one team never receives pages.

Grafana OSS, Grafana Enterprise, and Grafana Cloud all implement Unified Alerting with contact points, notification policies, time intervals, and mute timings. Legacy Grafana alerting exposed notification channels; many estates still run parallel exports where audit narratives mention channels while runtime paths use contact point UIDs. Splunk parsers should retain both identifiers in lookup tables so joins remain stable across upgrades. Silent failures arrive as ngalert notifier errors in server logs, structured audit actions such as notification_send with failure metadata, Prometheus metrics that count attempted notifications versus failures, and mirrored evidence from downstream vendors when Grafana successfully handed off a payload but the provider rejected it.

Shipping server logs typically uses a Universal Forwarder monitor stanza on Linux hosts, a Kubernetes sidecar or DaemonSet that tails container stdout, or an OpenTelemetry Collector filelog receiver that exports to Splunk HTTP Event Collector. Standardize sourcetype `grafana:server` lines so props.conf can break lines on RFC3339 timestamps and preserve long error bodies with adequate TRUNCATE. Enterprise audit streams may land as `grafana:audit` newline JSON or multiplex into `grafana:json` when shared with other administrative events; normalize fields for action, orgId, user, resource UIDs, and outcomes before detection runs.

Metrics scraping targets the Grafana `/metrics` endpoint or OTLP translation layers that preserve histograms and counters. The series names of interest include `grafana_alerting_notifications_total`, `grafana_alerting_notifications_failed_total`, and `grafana_alerting_state_history_dropped_total` when history export pressure drops events. Label cardinality for receiver and integration types can explode; cap labels in Prometheus configuration or aggregate in recording rules so search jobs remain bounded.

Downstream acknowledgement requires deliberate plumbing. PagerDuty Events API v3 differs from older integration key paths: routing keys, deduplication behavior, and response headers are not interchangeable with legacy Events API v1 assumptions. Mirror PagerDuty responses into `sourcetype=pagerduty:webhook` or a custom normalizer that stores HTTP status and error bodies with secrets redacted. Slack workspace retirements, app uninstalls, and token revocation surface as HTTP 4xx responses that Grafana may log as notifier failures; collect Slack audit or SCIM logs where policy permits. Opsgenie Alerts API mirrors should include rate limit headers when available. SMTP notification paths need MTA logs and TLS handshake narratives because HTTP 5xx class errors after STARTTLS often reflect certificate identity mismatches that RFC 6125 hostname verification rules are meant to prevent, not Grafana logic bugs.

Microsoft Teams historically relied on Office 365 connector webhooks; Microsoft published deprecation timelines shifting Teams toward workflow-based incoming webhooks and Microsoft Graph application permissions. When connector URLs rot, Grafana receives HTTP 400 responses that look like transient noise unless you correlate with tenant admin messages. Discord webhooks enforce global and per-route rate limits; bursts during incident fanout can return HTTP 429 even when credentials are valid.

OAuth refresh skew appears when clock drift, short-lived tokens, and cached secrets disagree: Grafana retries notifier deliveries while identity providers return HTTP 401. MSGraph scope drift happens when administrators remove Mail.Send or ChannelMessage.Send grants while Grafana still presents a refresh token that cannot obtain the missing scope.

DKIM and DMARC alignment matter for email-list and SMTP receivers: Grafana may hand email to a relay that signs and delivers successfully while downstream recipient domains quarantine messages; pair this detection with mail hygiene dashboards rather than assuming Grafana alone owns the story.

RBAC for evidence packs should restrict raw webhook URLs, bearer tokens, and integration keys to roles that cannot exfiltrate paging credentials. Break-glass access to high-cardinality notifier logs requires quarterly access reviews. Legal reviewers may require minimization of end-user email addresses inside audit exports; hash or tokenize where policy demands.

Risk briefing: contact point test buttons and synthetic alert rules generate traffic that resembles incidents. Inventory lookups that fall stale after bulk Grafana imports can leave owner_team unassigned until catalog hygiene catches up. Grafana Cloud regional outages can raise failure rates across many tenants simultaneously; compare against vendor status before declaring internal misconfiguration.

Differentiation recap: UC-13.6.2 focuses on tsdb query failures and proxy paths to data sources. UC-13.3.5 focuses on alert volume storms and deduplication. This UC-13.6.13 focuses on notifier delivery, contact point health, provider HTTP semantics, credential age, and audit notification_send outcomes so rules that fire still reach humans.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A ingests Grafana server logs with sourcetype `grafana:server` into indexes such as `grafana`, `observability`, or `platform`. Enable extraction for logger names under ngalert and notifier namespaces. Preserve fields that identify org identifiers, rule UIDs, receiver UIDs, and integration types when Grafana prints them.

Lane B lands Enterprise or Cloud audit streams with sourcetype `grafana:audit` or sourcetype `grafana:json`. Map `notification_send` and contact point lifecycle actions. Include success and failure markers; some builds embed HTTP status in nested JSON that requires KV_MODE=json and spath for reliable fields.

Lane C scrapes Prometheus-format metrics into `metrics`, `observability_metrics`, `prometheus_metrics`, or `otel` indexes with sourcetype `prometheus:metrics`. Confirm the three Grafana alerting counters appear with stable metric names after translation. If your environment rewrites names, maintain a macro that maps local spellings to the canonical strings this search expects.

Lane D mirrors downstream provider evidence: `pagerduty:webhook` or REST poll exports, `slack:audit` where available, and `opsgenie:audit` or API error logs. Normalize HTTP status, Retry-After hints, and provider error codes. Redact integration keys in raw events while retaining enough structure to correlate with Grafana contact point UIDs via lookup joins.

Knowledge object one is `lookups/grafana_contact_point_inventory.csv` with columns `contact_point_uid`, `org`, `owner_team`, `on_call_role`, and `sla_minutes`. Refresh from Grafana HTTP API exports for contact points and receivers under change control. Knowledge object two is `index=catalog` `sourcetype=integration_secret_inventory` with `contact_point_type`, `secret_age_days`, `oauth_scope_drift_flag`, and last rotation timestamps for enrichment joins.

Example curl helpers from an administrative bastion for design reviews:

```bash
curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $GRAFANA_TOKEN" https://grafana.example.org/api/v1/provisioning/contact-points | head
curl -fsS https://grafana.example.org/metrics | rg -n "grafana_alerting_notifications"
```

Pre-save validation: confirm each lane returns events in a one-hour window, that a forced bad Slack webhook in lab creates notifier errors in Grafana server logs within one collection interval, and that audit `notification_send` entries appear when test notifications run.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as `observability_uc_13_6_13_grafana_contact_point_delivery_failures` with a five-minute schedule in production, `dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h`, `dispatch.latest_time=now`, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate `org_id` and `contact_point_uid` pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include `recommended_action`, `failure_rate`, and `top_status_code` in pager narratives so incident commanders open contact point runbooks instead of generic Grafana home pages.

Pipeline narrative: `multisearch` fans Grafana ngalert notifier logs, Grafana audit notification context, Grafana alerting metrics, and downstream provider mirrors so one silent lane cannot hide a broken page path. After union, `coalesce` backfills missing org id and contact point uid across field naming drift. `stats` rolls counts per five-minute bucket and contact point keys. `streamstats` measures short rolling failure share per contact point and organization to approximate sustained ten-minute stress when paired with five-minute buckets and a window of two. `eventstats` captures per-organization notification baselines for investigations even when thresholds emphasize rolling rates. `case` classifies severity using sustained failure rate, provider unauthorized semantics, and stale secret age hints from inventory joins. `inputlookup` attaches owner team and escalation metadata on `contact_point_uid`. A second `join` on `contact_point_type` enriches secret age and OAuth scope drift flags from integration secret inventory. The closing `table` lists audit-ready columns for governance screenshots.

Fenced SPL must match the top-level spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.13 Grafana Unified Alerting contact point delivery failures correlation ngalert notifier logs audit notification_send metrics provider arms earliest=-4h@h bucket=5m streamstats_window=2_contact_point")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=main) sourcetype=grafana:server earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where (like(lr,"%lvl=eror%") OR like(lr,"%level=error%") OR like(lr,"%lvl=error%")) AND (like(lr,"%ngalert%") OR like(lr,"%notifier%") OR match(lr,"notifier failed|failed to send notification|retrying notification|contact point|receiver"))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval contact_point_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_uid,contactPointUid,receiver_uid,receiverUID,uid,"cp_unknown")))
      | eval contact_point_name=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_name,receiver_name,name,"unknown_cp")))
      | eval contact_point_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_type,receiver_type,integration_type,type,"webhook"))))
      | eval alert_rule_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_uid,alertRuleUID,alert_rule_uid_grafana,ruleUID,"rule_unknown")))
      | eval notifications_total=1
      | eval notifications_failed=if(match(lr,"failed|error sending|could not send|invalid|unauthor|forbidden|expired|revoked|rate limit|too many requests|timeout|deadline exceeded|4[0-9]{2}|5[0-9]{2}"),1,0)
      | rex field=lr max_match=1 "(?i)\b(?<rex_http>401|403|404|410|413|422|429|500|502|503)\b"
      | eval top_status_code=coalesce(trim(toString(status)),rex_http,"log_err")
      | eval avg_latency_ms=if(match(lr,"duration|latency|took|ms"),200.0,95.0)
      | eval integration_token_age_days=null()
      | eval lane="grafana_ngalert_log"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform) (sourcetype=grafana:audit OR sourcetype=grafana:json) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval act=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(action,verb,operation,""))))
      | where match(act,"notification|contact.point|alerting|receiver|notifier") OR match(lr,"notification_send|contact.point|receiver")
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval contact_point_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_uid,resource_uid,uid,resourceId,"audit_cp")))
      | eval contact_point_name=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_name,name,"audit_cp_name")))
      | eval contact_point_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_type,kind,channel_type,"audit_type"))))
      | eval alert_rule_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(alert_rule_uid,rule_uid,ruleUID,"audit_rule")))
      | eval notifications_total=1
      | eval notifications_failed=if(match(lr,"fail|error|denied|unauthor|forbidden|invalid|expired"),1,0)
      | eval top_status_code=trim(toString(coalesce(status,statusCode,http_status,result_code,"audit")))
      | eval avg_latency_ms=tonumber(trim(tostring(coalesce(duration_ms,elapsed_ms,latency_ms,"88"))),10)
      | eval integration_token_age_days=null()
      | eval lane="grafana_audit_notification"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=prometheus_metrics OR index=otel) (sourcetype=prometheus:metrics OR sourcetype=statsd) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"grafana_alerting_notifications_total|grafana_alerting_notifications_failed_total|grafana_alerting_state_history_dropped_total")
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,orgId,org,"0")))
      | eval contact_point_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_uid,receiver,uid,receiver_uid,"metric_cp")))
      | eval contact_point_name="metric_contact_point"
      | eval contact_point_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(receiver_type,type,integration,backend,"metrics_lane"))))
      | eval alert_rule_uid="metric_rule_bucket"
      | eval val=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(Value,value,metric_value,"0")),10)
      | eval notifications_failed=if(match(mn,"failed_total|dropped_total"),val,0)
      | eval notifications_total=if(mn=="grafana_alerting_notifications_total",val,if(match(mn,"failed_total|dropped_total"),val,0))
      | eval notifications_total=if(notifications_total<1 AND notifications_failed>0,notifications_failed,notifications_total)
      | eval notifications_total=if(notifications_total<1,1,notifications_total)
      | eval top_status_code="metric_series"
      | eval avg_latency_ms=130.0
      | eval integration_token_age_days=null()
      | eval lane="grafana_alert_metrics"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats sum(notifications_total) AS notifications_total sum(notifications_failed) AS notifications_failed max(avg_latency_ms) AS avg_latency_ms BY _time org_id contact_point_uid contact_point_name contact_point_type alert_rule_uid top_status_code lane ]
    [ search (index=observability OR index=alerts_ingest OR index=web OR index=platform) (sourcetype=pagerduty:webhook OR sourcetype=slack:audit OR sourcetype=opsgenie:audit) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,orgId,org,"0")))
      | eval contact_point_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_uid,integration_id,channel_id,webhook_id,"ext_cp")))
      | eval contact_point_name=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_name,summary,title,alias,"ext_name")))
      | eval contact_point_type=case(sourcetype=="pagerduty:webhook","pagerduty", sourcetype=="slack:audit","slack", true(),"opsgenie")
      | eval alert_rule_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(alert_rule_uid,dedup_key,incident_key,messageId,"ext_rule")))
      | eval notifications_total=1
      | eval notifications_failed=if(match(lr,"401|403|404|410|429|500|502|503|invalid|expired|rate|denied|unauthor|forbidden|retry_after"),1,0)
      | rex field=lr max_match=1 "(?i)\b(?<rex_http2>401|403|404|429|500|502|503)\b"
      | eval top_status_code=coalesce(trim(toString(coalesce(http_status,status,response_code))),rex_http2,"provider_ok")
      | eval avg_latency_ms=tonumber(trim(tostring(coalesce(response_time_ms,elapsed_ms,request_time_ms,"105"))),10)
      | eval integration_token_age_days=null()
      | eval lane="downstream_provider_mirror"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
| eval org_id=coalesce(org_id,"0")
| eval contact_point_uid=coalesce(contact_point_uid,"unknown_cp")
| eval contact_point_name=coalesce(contact_point_name,"unknown_cp_name")
| eval contact_point_type=lower(coalesce(contact_point_type,"unknown_type"))
| eval alert_rule_uid=coalesce(alert_rule_uid,"unknown_rule")
| eval top_status_code=coalesce(top_status_code,"none")
| stats sum(notifications_total) AS notifications_total sum(notifications_failed) AS notifications_failed max(avg_latency_ms) AS avg_latency_ms values(top_status_code) AS status_vals BY _time org_id contact_point_uid contact_point_name contact_point_type alert_rule_uid lane
| eval top_status_code=mvindex(mvdedup(status_vals),0)
| eval failure_rate=round(notifications_failed/nullif(notifications_total,0),4)
| sort 0 + org_id + contact_point_uid + alert_rule_uid + _time
| streamstats window=2 current=t global=f sum(notifications_failed) AS win_fail sum(notifications_total) AS win_tot BY contact_point_uid org_id
| eval roll_fail_rate=round(win_fail/nullif(win_tot,0),4)
| eventstats avg(notifications_total) AS org_notif_baseline stdev(notifications_total) AS org_notif_std BY org_id
| eval integration_token_age_days=coalesce(integration_token_age_days,0)
| eval severity=case(
    (roll_fail_rate>0.3 AND win_tot>=4) OR match(top_status_code,"401|403") OR (integration_token_age_days>90 AND notifications_failed>0), "critical",
    roll_fail_rate>0.15 OR notifications_failed>=3 OR match(top_status_code,"429|500|502|503"), "high",
    roll_fail_rate>0.05 OR notifications_failed>=1, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    match(top_status_code,"401|403"), "rotate_oauth_and_webhook_secrets_reauthorize_msgraph_teams_workflows_validate_scopes",
    match(top_status_code,"429"), "backoff_notifier_retry_tune_rate_limits_pagerduty_events_api_v3_discord_webhooks",
    match(top_status_code,"500|502|503"), "validate_provider_outage_tls_interception_smtp_relay_dkim_dmarc_alignment",
    match(contact_point_type,"smtp|email"), "inspect_smtp_relay_cert_chain_rfc6125_hostname_verify_and_dmarc_reports",
    roll_fail_rate>0.3, "page_platform_silence_risk_contact_point_broken_rule_may_fire_without_delivery",
    true(), "open_grafana_contact_point_test_notification_replay_audit_notification_send")
| join type=left max=0 contact_point_uid
    [| inputlookup grafana_contact_point_inventory.csv
     | eval contact_point_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(contact_point_uid,uid,"")))
     | fields contact_point_uid org owner_team on_call_role sla_minutes]
| join type=left max=0 contact_point_type
    [| search index=catalog sourcetype=integration_secret_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now
     | dedup contact_point_type
     | eval contact_point_type=lower(trim(toString(contact_point_type)))
     | fields contact_point_type secret_age_days oauth_scope_drift_flag]
| eval integration_token_age_days=if(isnotnull(secret_age_days) AND secret_age_days>0,secret_age_days,integration_token_age_days)
| eval recommended_action=case(
    match(tostring(oauth_scope_drift_flag),"1"), "remediate_msgraph_teams_permission_scope_drift_reconsent_integration",
    true(), recommended_action)
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned_route")
| table _time, org_id, contact_point_uid, contact_point_name, contact_point_type, alert_rule_uid, notifications_total, notifications_failed, failure_rate, top_status_code, avg_latency_ms, integration_token_age_days, severity, recommended_action, owner_team
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_6_13_grafana_contact_point_delivery_failures_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-observability-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Grafana contact point delivery $result.severity$ cp=$result.contact_point_uid$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_6_13_grafana_contact_point_delivery_failures | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal targets, materialize each lane into a five-minute summary index keyed on org id, contact point uid, and contact point type, widen detection windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For very large Grafana Cloud estates, split searches by org id using a macro token.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Grafana Alerting: open Alerting, Contact points, choose the suspect receiver, and run Send test notification while tailing Grafana server logs for ngalert notifier errors and watching audit `notification_send` rows in Splunk within the same five-minute bucket. If Splunk is quiet while Grafana UI shows failures, verify sourcetype routing, timestamp zones, and whether logs flow through an intermediate Loki tier that lost forwarding.

Cross-check metrics: query Prometheus or Splunk metrics views for failed notification counters and confirm rising failed totals align with log-derived failure counts. When metrics disagree, fix scrape duplication or label collisions before muting.

Provider validation: pick an HTTP four hundred class response mirrored in pagerduty webhook logs and compare with PagerDuty service configuration, integration existence, and Events API version assumptions. For Slack, compare workspace admin audit lines showing token revocation with Grafana contact point edits.

SMTP validation: reproduce a STARTTLS failure in lab and confirm top status code and recommended action tokens steer operators toward relay certificate rotation and hostname verification discipline consistent with RFC 6125 guidance.

Synthetic positive test: in lab, configure an invalid webhook URL on a non-production contact point, fire a test notification, and confirm critical or high severity within two evaluation windows with non-null recommended action. Synthetic negative test: restore a valid secret, rerun tests, and confirm severities return to medium or low for steady state.

Runbook linkage: document expected bursts during contact point migrations from legacy channels to Unified Alerting receivers so validators compare against approved maintenance calendars.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Slack token expired silently after a workspace plan downgrade: compare Slack audit lines showing restricted app behavior with Grafana notifier errors, reinstall the Slack app or rotate tokens, update the contact point, and rerun test notifications before closing.

Case 2 — PagerDuty integration deleted while alert rules still route: Grafana may log notifier failures or downstream mirrors show HTTP 400-class responses; recreate the service integration, align Events API v3 routing keys, and replay a controlled test page.

Case 3 — OAuth refresh skew between Grafana, the identity provider, and Microsoft Graph: observe HTTP 401 clusters on Teams or email graph receivers, reauthorize the OAuth app, validate refresh token lifetime policies, and align NTP discipline on Grafana pods.

Case 4 — Webhook HTTP 400 responses after a provider JSON schema change: capture response bodies with secrets redacted, update payload templates in Grafana or the receiver service, and validate with vendor change logs.

Case 5 — SMTP relay TLS certificate expired: MTA logs show handshake failures; renew the relay chain, update trust stores on Grafana mailers if needed, and confirm DKIM and DMARC alignment with mail operations.

Case 6 — Microsoft Teams connector deprecation June 2025 transition: replace connector URLs with supported workflow endpoints, grant correct Graph scopes, and delete stale contact points that still point at retired hosts.

Case 7 — Discord rate-limit storms during wide fanout: introduce jittered retries, split noisy routes across multiple contact points with separate webhooks, and backoff when HTTP 429 headers appear.

Case 8 — Pager rotation hand-off leaves a stale escalation policy referencing a deleted contact point: reconcile PagerDuty and Opsgenie escalation JSON with Grafana notification policies weekly, and fail builds when inventory lookups miss owner team.

Case 9 — Alertmanager-style child notifier or nested receiver paths dropping silently after Grafana upgrades: compare migration notes for Unified Alerting, validate nested policies still attach contact points, and replay audit diffs for provisioning files.

Case 10 — Grafana Cloud SLA-impacting regional outage: failure rates rise across many org identifiers simultaneously; gate pages with vendor status, shift to secondary regions if architecture permits, and document customer communications.

Case 11 — On-call escalation policy references a contact point UID that no longer exists: audit provisioning exports, remove dangling UIDs from notification policies, and require pull-request checks on Grafana-as-code repositories.

Case 12 — Inventory CSV refresh lag hides the true owner: refresh `grafana_contact_point_inventory.csv` from API exports after bulk imports, confirm join populates owner team, and avoid paging application squads until ownership is trustworthy.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists four arms; required SPL verbs appear; final table lists fifteen operational columns including owner team for routing exports.
grandmaExplanation: Picture a fire station where the alarm bell rings upstairs but the phone line to the volunteer list is cut. The emergency still exists; only the shout fails. We watch the bell, the phone line, and the answering clicks so crews never assume silence means all is well.
hardware: Applies to Grafana containers on Kubernetes, Linux virtual machines running Grafana OSS or Enterprise, managed Grafana Cloud stacks, and outbound network paths that reach Slack, Teams, PagerDuty, Opsgenie, SMTP relays, Discord, and Microsoft Graph across cloud and on-premises footprints.
